nameMeanings.name: Greer
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish (Lanarkshire): from the medieval Scottish personal name Grier, a vernacular form of Gregor (see Gregory and compare Grierson ). This form of the surname is common in Ireland as well as Scotland.

History: A Scottish family by the name of Grierson claim descent from Gilbert, a son of Malcolm MacGregor (died 1374), 11th Lord of MacGregor. Gilbert was known as both MacGregor and Gregorson. In 1400 he was granted lands by the Earl of March in the name Grierson. The numerous northern Irish bearers of the name Greer claim descent from this same Gilbert.

variants.name: Gregor
meanings: English (Cornwall) and Scottish (Midlothian and Aberdeenshire): from the personal name Gregor, from Latin Gregorius (see Gregory ). Compare Greer .

German, Slovak, Slovenian, and Polish; Sorbian (Gŕegor): from the personal name Gregor, Lower Sorbian Gŕegor, a vernacular form (in Polish a rare variant of Grzegorz, in Lower Sorbian a variant of Grogor; see 3 below) of Latin Gregorius (see Gregory ). The German surname has also been adopted by Czechs (compare Rehor ) and Ashkenazic Jews. In North America, this surname may also be a shortened form of Slavic patronymics like Slovenian Gregorc, Gregorič (see Gregorich ), and Gregorčič, and Slovak Gregorovič.

Germanized or Americanized form of Polish Grzegorz and Sorbian Grogor or Hrjehoŕ: from the personal name Polish Grzegorz, Lower Sorbian Grogor, Upper Sorbian Hrjehoŕ, equivalents of Latin Gregorius (see Gregory ).

nameMeanings.name: Anne
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: variant of Anne , in common use as a given name in most European languages. Among people with a classical education, it has from time to time been associated with Virgil's Aeneid, where it is borne by the sister of Dido , Queen of Carthage. This Phoenician name may ultimately be of Semitic origin, and thus related to the biblical Anne. However, the connection, if it exists, is indirect rather than direct.

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: When Anne Greer was born in 1400, in Westphalia, Prussia, her father, John Greer, was 20 and her mother, Nancy  Ann Walker, was 20. She had at least 3 sons and 1 daughter with Richard Ashe de Esse. She died in 1460, in Devon, England, at the age of 60.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Anne Greer est née en 1400, à Provinz Westfalen, Prusse, son père, John Greer, avait 20 ans et sa mère, Nancy  Ann Walker, avait 20 ans. Elle a eu au moins 3 fils et 1 fille avec Richard Ashe d’Esse. Elle est décédée en 1460, à Devon, Angleterre, à l’âge de 60 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Anne Greer 1400 in Provinz Westfalen, Preußen geboren wurde, war ihr Vater John Greer 20 und ihre Mutter Nancy  Ann Walker 20. Sie hatte mit Richard Ashe de Esse mindestens 3 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Sie starb 1460 im Alter von 60 Jahren in Devon, England.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Anne Greer è nata il nel 1400 nel Provinz Westfalen, Preußen, sua padre, John Greer, aveva 20 anni e sua madre, Nancy  Ann Walker, aveva 20 anni. Lei avuto almeno 3 figli e 1 figlia con Richard Ashe de Esse. Morì nel 1460, nel Devon, England, all'età di 60 anni.

ko.lifeSummary: Anne Greer(이)가 1400년 플레우ss 플로빈츠 웨스트파렌에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 John Greer(은)는 20세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Nancy  Ann Walker(은)는 20세였다. 그녀는 Richard Ashe de Esse(와)과 적어도 세 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1460년 영국 데폰에서 60세의 나이로 사망했다.
